parsed_review.summary: CP00-838 (RP27 Platform Extensibility — Hermes evidence secondary workflow bridge, descriptor-only, risk A, 10 units RP27.P08.M04.S06-S15) is supported by its implementation, tests, local validation evidence, and no-write/no-real-data boundaries. Verified through Read/Grep/Glob only: (1) Plan, manifest, baseline, contract latest_projection, contract projections.cp838, and registry all agree on the 10 units, micro_title "Secondary Workflow Slice", and deliverable distribution {hermes_evidence:5, claude_review:1, implementation:4}; deviation_from_plan:false. Registry builds the rows from HERMES_VALIDATION_ROWS.slice(5,15), which yields exactly the 10 expected titles (blocked-claim, permission/audit/no-real-data receipts, Claude dependency marker, human approval marker, PASS / PASS_WITH_FINDINGS / BLOCK semantics, evidence template). (2) Descriptor-only and runtime-closed: PLATFORM_EXTENSIBILITY_NO_WRITE_ATTESTATION (all-false flags) is spread into the descriptor and every case-set row carries descriptor_only:true / runtime_execution:false with all leak flags false; validators.js rejects any api_key_issued/webhook_delivered/workflow_executed/permission_decision_written/audit_event_written/writes_product_state and enforces the no-secret/no-real-data/no-audit-body guards. (3) Authority boundary intact: claude_is_final_approval:false, local_validation_claims_enterprise_trust:false, human_final_approval_required_for_runtime_opening:true, runtime opening deferred to CP00-839; this matches the receipt's authority boundary and keeps Claude a read-only reviewer, not final approver. (4) Hermes control-plane pattern used only for deterministic review packet/receipt: hermes_packet.emits_runtime_receipt:false and command_matrix is validation-only; fixture_summary states no runtime command execution/permission/audit/extension behavior is opened. (5) Claude packet read_only:true, promotes_claude_to_final_approval:false, tools Read/Grep/Glob, with hardening blockers (not_logged_in, malformed_json, usage_limit_error, permission_denial, write_tool_used, runtime_receipt_claim). (6) Closeout handoff routes to CP00-839 / RP27.P08.M04.S16 with runtime_opening_allowed:false. Tests bind the registry to the live plan and assert runtime-closed descriptor + projection currency + CP00-837 historical preservation; the validate script checks upstream lineage to CP00-837. The pending-review placeholders (production_ready:false, runtime_ready:false, PENDING_REVIEW in adjudication/inspection/claude-review-result) are the correct pre-receipt state, with valid_review_receipts:[] and invalid_review_attempts:[] — no invalid evidence is presented as valid, and no auth-failure/0-byte/malformed/fenced/tool-call-shaped output is being relied upon. The production_ready claim here is the descriptor-verified flag only; runtime_ready stays false and enterprise trust remains human-gated, so the layered claim is sound. Note: under the read-only constraint I did not independently re-execute the validation commands; I confirmed the implementation is internally consistent with the recorded exit_code 0 results. One non-blocking P3 traceability nit in command-evidence.json (gate command_ids vs recorded commands mismatch). No P0/P1/P2 findings. Verdict: PASS; does not block pack or this-CP goal closeout.

findings.title: command-evidence hermes_gate.command_ids do not match the recorded commands
findings.details: hermes_gate.command_ids lists control-plane identifiers (rp00_control_plane_contract_validate, spec_requirement_validate) that do not correspond to the executed commands array, which records only rp27_platform_extensibility_validate and platform_model_tests (both exit_code 0). The evidence file therefore does not self-document which gate commands produced the recorded exit codes. This is purely a traceability/documentation nit in a pack whose subject matter is evidence receipts; it does not affect the descriptor-only, no-write, no-real-data, authority-boundary, or runtime-closed claims, all of which are independently verified in the contract, registry, validators, and tests.
findings.recommendation: Align hermes_gate.command_ids with the commands actually recorded, or add the referenced control-plane/spec-requirements command results, so the evidence file is internally traceable.
findings.post_review_disposition: Noted as non-blocking P3. Does not affect any gate or the production_ready (descriptor-verified) claim; no change required for this pack to close. Recommend tidying in a future evidence-template pass.
